No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger, food security, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to health, well-being (beyond general confidence), or disease prevention.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (4.3), develops professional communication skills for employment (4.4), and explicitly covers 'sustainability issues' (4.7). Model B's original reasoning was most comprehensive.
evidence: Business Writing and Oral Training I
Students will acquire professional communication skills.

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to gender-specific issues, discrimination, or empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access, renewables, or efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's focus on 'Business Writing & Oral Training' and 'professional communication skills' directly supports productive employment and decent work (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to industry, infrastructure development, or specific innovation.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Developing communication skills and confidence can indirectly empower individuals, broadly supporting social and economic inclusion (Target 10.2), though the link is weak.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to urban development, housing, or community sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competence B explicitly states students will 'understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' directly supporting awareness for sustainable development (Target 12.8).

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, there's no specific evidence linking the course to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's focus on 'Assertive Communication' and 'Active Listening' can foster skills for more participatory and inclusive decision-making (Target 16.7).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly aims to 'develop team spirit' and for students to 'work effectively in teams,' which are foundational skills for effective partnerships (Target 17.17).
